message:
fix(glamour): add backtick delimiters to ASCII style inline code

The ASCII style for terminal output was missing inline code delimiters,
causing code spans to render without visible boundaries. Adds prefix
and suffix backticks to the code StylePrimitive within ascii_style().

This makes inline code like \`example\` render as \`example\` in ASCII
mode, matching the expected markdown-in-terminal rendering convention.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -722,7 +722,7 @@ pub fn ascii_style() -> StyleConfig {
         enumeration: StylePrimitive::new().block_prefix(". "),
         task: StyleTask::new().ticked("[x] ").unticked("[ ] "),
         image_text: StylePrimitive::new().format("Image: {{.text}} →"),
-        code: StyleBlock::new(),
+        code: StyleBlock::new().style(StylePrimitive::new().prefix("`").suffix("`")),
         code_block: StyleCodeBlock::new().block(StyleBlock::new().margin(DEFAULT_MARGIN)),
         table: StyleTable::new().separators("|", "|", "-"),
         definition_description: StylePrimitive::new().block_prefix("\n* "),
